title: Quiz: Surface Chemistry and Adsorption
description: Test your understanding of surface chemistry concepts

questions.question: How is surface energy calculated from a slab model?
questions.explanation: Surface energy γ = (E_slab - N×E_bulk)/(2A), where the factor of 2 accounts for both surfaces of the slab. This gives the excess energy per unit area due to creating the surface.

questions.question: According to d-band theory, what happens when the d-band center moves up (toward Fermi level)?
questions.explanation: A higher d-band center (closer to Fermi level) means more d-states are available for bonding with adsorbates, leading to stronger adsorption. This is why under-coordinated sites and strained surfaces are more reactive.

questions.question: Why is vacuum needed in slab calculations?

options.text: To prevent interaction between periodic images
To increase accuracy of bulk calculation

questions.explanation: Vacuum (typically 12-15 Å) is needed to prevent artificial interaction between the surface and its periodic image in the z-direction. Without vacuum, the slab would behave like a bulk material.

questions.question: What typically happens to adsorption energy as surface coverage increases?
questions.explanation: Due to repulsive lateral interactions between adsorbates (dipole-dipole and electronic through-bond), adsorption energy typically becomes less negative (weaker binding) as coverage increases.

questions.question: The FCC(111) surface is more reactive than FCC(110) because it has higher coordination.
questions.explanation: False. Higher coordination typically means LOWER reactivity (d-band further from Fermi). FCC(110) has steps/edges with lower coordination, making it more reactive than the close-packed FCC(111).

questions.question: Which factors affect the d-band center? (Select all)
questions.explanation: The d-band center is affected by coordination (lower = higher ε_d), strain (tensile = higher ε_d), and alloying (electron count and orbital overlap changes). DFT temperature is usually 0K and doesn't significantly affect ε_d.
